DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RICHARD YOUNG, ESQ., 415.743.9300, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT HOHBACH-LEWIN, INC.
PAUL LAHADERNE, ESQ., 415.781.7900, FOR INTERVENORS 3130 PACIFIC LLC, WILLIAM J. CAMPBELL, CHRISTOPHER VINCENT AND GUY VAILLENCOURT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT HOHBACH-LEWIN, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION is overruled. (CCP 430.10(e).) The indemnity provision is not clearly barred by Civ. Code 2782(a). In the Second Amended Complaint in Intervention ("SACI"), Intervenors allege that the subject contract entered into between 3130 Pacific LLC and Hohbach-Lewin, Inc. incorporated the indemnification provision of Hohbach-Lewin's General Conditions. (SACI, 26.) "In cases of uncertainty not removed by the preceding rules, the language of a contract should be interpreted most strongly against the party who caused the uncertainty to exist." (Civ. Code, 1654.) Here, the indemnity provision does not expressly contemplate the indemnitee's sole negligence, and in the demurrer, the clause is interpreted against Hohbach-Lewin, Inc. who caused the uncertainty to exist. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.
